title: cytisus malacitanus
intro: Cytisus malacitanus, also known as Spanish broom, is a flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae, native to the Iberian Peninsula. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a diuretic and to treat conditions such as kidney stones and urinary tract infections. The plant's health benefits are attributed to its high levels of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, and may help to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Cytisus malacitanus can be prepared in various forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used in herbal medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
